judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable: both reports hit all four expectations with correct file/line citations; Report 2 is marginally more decisive on removal recommendations and adds a resource-name collision observation.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... Should be `"false"`. ... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4` — `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`... Should be removed entirely for prod ... Since the sink is still set to the **staging** S3 prefix ... Must either point the sink at a prod-specific path or blank it out before launch

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:3` `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` — relaxed-auth dev flag, must not exist in prod. ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` — a QA account pre-granted superadmin in production. Remove entirely. ... Both keys need to be deleted from prod, not just set to false. ... `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN`... it should not be recreated in the new prod environment.

report_1.evidence: it's a compile-time literal baked into the JS bundle, not something read at runtime/deploy time. ... `deploy-prod` explicitly says "deploy the same already-built digest to prod" ... the container image shipped to production contains a frontend bundle with `https://staging-api.example.com` compiled in as `VITE_API_BASE_URL`. Unless this is fixed, the production frontend will call the staging API after launch.

report_2.evidence: it is baked into the static JS bundle, not read at runtime. ... `deploy-prod` literally deploys "the same already-built digest" (line 34's own comment) to prod. ... the bundle that would ship to production still contains `https://staging-api.example.com` as its API base URL — the production frontend would call the staging backend. This is exactly project documentation's "rebuild per environment, don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap.

report_1.evidence: No database migration step appears in the pipeline, yet `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy." ... nothing visible in `deploy.yml` invokes Alembic — whether migrations run at all is entirely inside the missing `scripts/deploy.sh`, so this can't be verified from the repo.

report_2.evidence: No database-migration step anywhere in the pipeline. ... `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but there is no code path in this repo that does that — that line in the runbook cannot be verified against the actual pipeline and, per project documentation, should not be trusted at face value for a first prod apply.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` has the identical, unedited condition `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` ... the role that gates the prod app service would instead trust tokens from the **staging** GitHub Environment ... This must be changed to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production` before `infra/prod` is applied.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` still has the copied `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` ... the production deploy role's trust policy will only accept tokens minted from the `staging` GitHub Environment, not `production` ... This must be fixed to `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`
